issues.message: Concept declared the garden map at /visit/map, but the builder shipped it as garden-map.html at the root (with a separate visit.html for hours/admission). The intent — an interactive SVG garden map with right-rail zone detail, legend, and printed-map link — is fully satisfied by garden-map.html, so this is a route-naming difference rather than a missing page.

Three pages (Encyclopedia index, Field Notes, Shop) have unlabeled form controls — the encyclopedia search box, the Field Notes 'subscribe to the printed almanac' email input, and the Shop waitlist email input lack associated <label> elements or aria-label attributes.

On the Encyclopedia index, the metric flagged each cultivar plate card as 'fewer_than_two_children'. Visually the 12 cards are highly consistent (illustration, binomial, common name, family, badges); this looks like a structural-detection artifact, not a real layout problem.

Layout-consistency scores dip on the cultivar entry (45) and membership (58) pages. Both are intentionally editorial — two-column herbarium plate vs. ruled prose, tier table with varying internal benefit lists — so the asymmetry is by design and reads well in the screenshots.

repair_instructions: Add accessible labels to the three unlabeled form inputs: (1) in encyclopedia.html, give the cultivar search input either a visible <label> or an aria-label such as aria-label="Search the cultivar encyclopedia"; (2) in field-notes.html, label the 'Printed Almanac' subscribe email input (aria-label="Email address for the printed almanac"); (3) in shop.html, label the waitlist email input (aria-label="Email address for the nursery waitlist"). Keep the visual design unchanged.
